questions.question: A hiker's altitude $h$ in meters after $t$ hours is modeled by $h(t) = -2(t - 3)^2 + 1000$. What is the vertex of this parabola?
The fuel consumption $F$ in liters of a vehicle after traveling $x$ kilometers is given by $F(x) = 0.02(x - 50)^2 + 20$. What is the minimum fuel consumption?
A park ranger tracks the population $P$ of a species after $t$ years, modeled by $P(t) = -5(t - 2)(t + 1)$. What is the $t$-intercept of this quadratic function?
The cost $C$ in dollars of maintaining a hiking trail after $x$ months is given by $C(x) = 0.1(x - 10)^2 + 50$. What is the vertex form of this quadratic function?
The depth $d$ in meters of a lake after $x$ meters from the shore is modeled by $d(x) = -0.2(x - 20)^2 + 10$. What is the $x$-intercept of this quadratic function?

A group of friends want to go on a hot air balloon ride. The height $h$ in meters of the balloon after $t$ minutes is given by $h(t) = -0.5(t - 5)^2 + 200$. What is the maximum height reached by the balloon?
A wildlife conservation organization tracks the population $P$ of an endangered species after $x$ years, modeled by $P(x) = -2(x - 3)(x + 2)$. What is the vertex form of this quadratic function?
The temperature $T$ in degrees Celsius at a certain depth $x$ in meters is given by $T(x) = -0.01(x - 100)^2 + 20$. What is the temperature at $x = 50$ meters?

The population $P$ of a species of birds after $t$ years is modeled by $P(t) = -3(t - 2)(t + 1)$. Find the $t$-intercepts, vertex, and axis of symmetry of this quadratic function. Show your work and explain your answer.

A company offers a $x$-kilometer hiking trip. The cost $C$ in dollars of the trip is given by $C(x) = 0.05(x - 10)^2 + 200$. Find the vertex form of this quadratic function, and interpret the meaning of the vertex in the context of the problem. Show your work and explain your answer.

teacher_rules: Make sure students show their work for FRQs
Emphasize the importance of interpreting parameters in vertex and intercept form
Allow students to use graphing calculators to check their answers
